lede: A look at December 9 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Carlo Azeglio Ciampi and Peter Kropotkin, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Park Geun-hye
events.desc: President Park Geun-hye of South Korea is impeached by the country's National Assembly in response to a major political scandal.

events.title: Somalia
events.desc: American troops land in Somalia for Operation Restore Hope.

events.title: William P. Rogers
events.desc: U.S. Secretary of State William P. Rogers proposes his plan for a ceasefire in the War of Attrition; Egypt and Jordan accept it over the objections of the PLO, which leads to civil war in Jordan in September 1970.

events.title: Douglas Engelbart
events.desc: Douglas Engelbart gave what became known as "The Mother of All Demos", publicly debuting the computer mouse, hypertext, and the bit-mapped graphical user interface using the oN-Line System (NLS).

events.title: Kecksburg UFO incident
events.desc: Kecksburg UFO incident: A fireball is seen from Michigan to Pennsylvania; with witnesses reporting something crashing in the woods near Pittsburgh.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Operation Compass: British and Indian troops under the command of Major-General Richard O'Connor attack Italian forces near Sidi Barrani in Egypt.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Field Marshal Allenby captures Jerusalem from the Ottoman Empire.

events.title: Cross Mountain Mine disaster
events.desc: A mine explosion near Briceville, Tennessee, kills 84 miners despite rescue efforts led by the United States Bureau of Mines.

events.title: Iran
events.desc: The Iranian city of Bushehr surrenders to occupying British forces.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British troops and Loyalists, misinformed about Patriot militia strength, lose the Battle of Great Bridge, ending British rule in Virginia.

births.name: Carlo Azeglio Ciampi
births.line: Italian economist and politician, 10th President of Italy

births.name: Peter Kropotkin
births.line: Russian zoologist, economist, geographer, and philosopher

births.name: Stefen Fangmeier
births.line: American visual effects designer and director

births.name: Grace Hopper
births.line: American admiral and computer scientist, designed COBOL

deaths.text: Karl Blossfeldt, German photographer, sculptor, and educator
Heinrich Christian Friedrich Schumacher, Danish surgeon, botanist, and academic
Sigismund, Holy Roman Emperor, King of Hungary from 1387 to 1437, Holy Roman Emperor from 1433

observances.text: Anna's Day, marks the day to start the preparation process of the lutefisk to be consumed on Christmas Eve, as well as a Swedish name day, celebrating all people named Anna. (Sweden and Finland)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Conception of the Most Holy Theotokos by St. Anne (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Hannah (biblical figure) (Eastern Orthodox Church)
